scope.summary: Make doctor distinguish an intentionally absent signed auditor from a configured signing source or the fail-closed no-key startup refusal; do not infer an active auditor from the default audit path.
out_of_scope: Implementing the separate B8c unsigned refusal/security-event trail.

entry_points.name: doctor check 13 audit/state layout
entry_points.trace: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/target/ws-b-proof cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib doctor::tests::audit_posture_is_not_inferred_from_the_default_audit_path -- --exact: 1 passed in a clean detached worktree at cc119ba82c528aa1457c10db5d77c46aa15e68eb. The test asserts keyless read-only output is audit: DISABLED with Skip status, a configured signer is described as configuration rather than an active auditor, and keyless writable output is Fail with ORACLEMCP_AUDIT_KEY_REQUIRED.

entry_points.name: binary startup-policy mapping
entry_points.trace: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/target/ws-b-proof cargo test -p oraclemcp --bin oraclemcp tests::doctor_audit_posture_matches_startup_audit_policy_without_opening_a_log -- --exact: 1 passed in a clean detached worktree at cc119ba82c528aa1457c10db5d77c46aa15e68eb. The test covers read-only DISABLED, writable no-key refusal, and a configured signing source without opening an audit log.

limitations: No live Oracle result is claimed.
follow_ups: oraclemcp-091-b8c-refusal-trail-rhg0m remains open; doctor must add the explicitly labeled unsigned refusal-trail status only when that feature lands.
